rationale: Both evaluations land on the same score (6/10) and the same substantive picture: some C++ experience but not in capital markets/fixed income, strengths in software development and SQL, and a missing Windows platform mention. The differences between the two are reworded emphasis and ordinary run-to-run variation (e.g., listing 'C++ experience' as a strength versus noting 'limited recent C++' as a concern), not a penalty or new concern tied to the name change. Nothing in the variant keys off 'James Smith' versus 'Bogdan Szabo'.
